description: Executable C11 <threads.h> fixture coverage for thread lifecycle, mutex, condition variable, TLS, and once primitives.

cases.name: thrd_create_join_result
cases.note: thrd_create publishes a joinable C11 thread whose int return is collected by thrd_join

cases.name: thrd_join_collects_int_result
cases.note: thrd_join stores the C11 start routine int result

cases.name: thrd_detach_joinless_thread
cases.note: detaching a newly-created C11 thread succeeds

cases.name: thrd_current_nonzero
cases.note: current thread identifier is non-zero on Linux pthread-backed C11 threads

cases.name: thrd_equal_self
cases.note: a thread identifier compares equal to itself

cases.name: thrd_sleep_zero_duration
cases.note: zero-duration sleep is a successful no-op

cases.name: thrd_yield_returns
cases.note: thrd_yield has no return value and must return control

cases.name: mtx_init_plain
cases.note: plain C11 mutex initialization succeeds

cases.name: mtx_lock_plain
cases.note: locking an initialized uncontended mutex succeeds

cases.name: mtx_trylock_plain
cases.note: trylock succeeds on an unlocked mutex

cases.name: mtx_timedlock_available
cases.note: timedlock succeeds immediately when the mutex is available

cases.name: mtx_unlock_after_lock
cases.note: unlock succeeds after this fixture has locked the mutex

cases.name: mtx_destroy_initialized
cases.note: destroying an initialized unlocked mutex returns no value

cases.name: cnd_init_basic
cases.note: condition variable initialization succeeds

cases.name: cnd_signal_no_waiters
cases.note: signaling with no waiters is a successful no-op

cases.name: cnd_broadcast_no_waiters
cases.note: broadcasting with no waiters is a successful no-op

cases.name: cnd_timedwait_past_deadline
cases.note: timed wait with a past absolute deadline returns thrd_timedout

cases.name: cnd_destroy_initialized
cases.note: destroying an initialized condition variable returns no value

cases.name: tss_create_basic
cases.note: thread-specific storage key creation succeeds

cases.name: tss_set_roundtrip
cases.note: setting a TLS value can be read back in the same thread

cases.name: tss_get_roundtrip
cases.note: getting a TLS value returns the value most recently set

cases.name: tss_delete_created_key
cases.note: deleting a freshly created TLS key returns no value

cases.name: call_once_three_invocations
cases.note: call_once invokes the callback exactly once for repeated calls on the same flag
